title: Chapter Ninety: One Technique
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

She had been watching from the rooftop.

The Seventh Apostle had activated her blood technique's sensory path -- sharpening her hearing to capture every strand of Yeon-shin's breathing. She had heard everything. Including the Namgung woman's words.

*I'll let you just wash my feet.*

The words polluted her ears. She moved before thought could catch up -- the way she always moved, the moment she made up her mind. Through the inn's entrance. Through the silence her arrival created. Namgung Mi's powdered cheek pressing against her fingers felt disgusting, but she could not furrow her brow.

The boy was watching.

*My small noble Grand Teacher. Your dignity is my prestige.*

"What did you say?" she asked again, pressing harder.

Namgung Mi could not answer. Her blood points were already sealed. She could only stare with wide, pain-filled eyes while the fragments of the inn's shattered door clattered to the ground behind the Seventh Apostle.

Silence fell over every soul in the room.

Yeon-shin assessed the situation in the space between heartbeats. The Seventh Apostle in her pure blood robe, energy waves pressing against every person in the inn like a hand on the chest. No mark of Blood Flame Sect, but the crimson fabric and ominous presence were unmistakable.

*If Namgung Mi dies here, the responsibility transfers to Desolate Sect. We lose the justification for the martial matches.*

He sent a sound transmission.

*--You. What are you doing?*

No answer. He stared at the Seventh Apostle's profile. She showed only her side, refusing to meet his gaze. But the hand gripping Namgung Mi's face stopped pressing harder.

*She's listening.*

*--It was a promise made on our names. You've blatantly broken it. Three hundred zhang has become one step.*

Silence.

*--I'm very disappointed, Seventh Apostle.*

Her shoulders shrank.

It was an unbelievable sight. The woman whose hands had killed more people than Yeon-shin could count -- her slender frame visible for an instant as the mighty energy waves faltered around her. She looked small. The pure blood robe hung loose on her narrow shoulders.

*Is it a bluff?*

He was already raising the Radiant Demon Technique. Semi-transparent paths appeared in his mind -- sword trajectories extending toward her body. Every one of them crumbled before reaching her. No technique in Radiant Sword Style could land an effective hit with a single strike.

*Must coordinate with Young Hero Namgung.*

Behind him, Hwa-sin's energy waves bloomed with killing intent. "Don't worry about half a second's difference," the White Qilin said quietly. "I'll support right behind."

Yeon-shin placed his hand on the Desolate Sword's hilt. Lifted his right heel. A common sword-drawing stance, but the Jeong family dynamic technique's solemn energy wrapped around everything within five steps.

*--Are you saying to forget the martial arts exchange promise? Twelve seconds remain in our agreement for Demon Roaring Blood Technique and one strike. You mean to cut everything off with your own hands?*

"No..."

The Seventh Apostle's voice came out hazy. Her head tilted downward. It was detestable -- like Namgung Mi's cruelty, but inverted. A mass murderer performing contrition before a boy she obsessed over while holding a woman's face in a grip that could crush bone.

*--That's what your behaviour now means.*

She moved.

SWISH!

One hand turned Namgung Mi's body and grabbed her nape. The motion was instant, fluid, a technique sequence that looked like flowing water. She slammed Namgung Mi's head down onto the table.

BOOM!

"Because of this," she said. The smile had vanished from her lips.

And then she turned. Stepped lightly. Walked out of the inn with the bearing of someone who had been defeated, though no blade had touched her.

*--You must come again. I'll let you land one technique.*

Her sound transmission faded with her presence -- rising to the rooftop, drifting away, dissolving into the air like smoke.

Yeon-shin stood with his hand on the lowered Desolate Sword.

*If those words are true, I should cut off her right arm first. She won't give up her neck.*

Around them, the inn's patrons erupted into chatter.

"The Desolate Sect young master drove away the evil sect spawn--"

"That hair was black. Surely not an Apostle--"

"Weren't the pupils red...?"

None of them approached. The crowd that had stayed through the entire confrontation -- too brave or too foolish to flee -- watched from a distance that said they understood the difference between spectating and involvement.

*I controlled the Seventh Apostle?* The thought was absurd. Unacceptable.

*I must stand on martial might alone. Relying on an enemy's goodwill has limits. This is beyond common sense.*

Hwa-sin appeared at his side, sword still drawn. His expression was complicated as he looked at Namgung Mi sprawled across the broken table.

"We should find a physician. Left like this, she'll have trouble standing for the martial matches."

"...Yes. We should."

They could not leave Huangzhou right away. Four days passed. A physician was found. Namgung Mi's groans echoed through their rented quarters at night. Only Hwa-sin reacted to them.

*Is even an enemy still blood kin?*

Yeon-shin lay in the dark, absorbed in deeper meditation than he had ever attempted. Palm techniques. Cultivation methods. The architecture of new martial arts forming behind his closed eyes. He worked until dawn light swept through the window like a grey blade.

On the fifth morning, the noise came.

It was not the noise of a crowd gathering. It was the noise of a crowd *responding* -- to energy waves and presence so immense that the air itself seemed to part. Even Namgung Mi's vitality surged. She looked at Yeon-shin from the bed with a sneer that said, *Whatever your identity, you must listen to my words now.*

From the street below, voices rose like a tide.

"Azure Qilin! It's Young Lord Azure Qilin Namgung!"

"Namgung Se-jin in Huangzhou--?"

"That inn! Weren't there Desolate Sect warriors there!"

The Namgung family's young master had come for his sister.

Yeon-shin placed his hand on his sword.

*So the board changes again.*
